_comment: Exactly MAX_ROWS (10,000, see apps/api/src/routes/importExport.ts) valid, distinct rows -- every national_id is checksum-valid and unique (prefix range 2,000,000-2,009,999, disjoint from every other fixture/test in this repo). MAX_ROWS itself is enforced at the ROUTE (POST /import/workers), not inside CsvImportService.importCsv/parseWorkersCsv -- so this fixture's own service-level expectation is simply 'succeeds fully, right at the documented cap'. It is intended primarily for route-level (supertest) and load-test (apps/api/loadtest/) use, where the MAX_ROWS check itself is exercised.
call: CsvImportService.importCsv(csvText, companyId) OR POST /import/workers (route-level, where MAX_ROWS is actually enforced)

expectRoute.note: at exactly MAX_ROWS, the route must accept and enqueue the job, not reject it
